mnemonics.title: Tree Traversal Order
mnemonics.content: Preorder: Root, Left, Right (R-L-R) | Inorder: Left, Root, Right (L-R-R) | Postorder: Left, Right, Root (L-R-R) | Remember: PReorder = Root FIRST | INorder = Root IN middle | POSTorder = Root LAST

mnemonics.title: Binary Tree Node Count
mnemonics.content: Full Tree: 2^(h+1) - 1 nodes | Minimum nodes with L leaves: 2L - 1 | Remember: FULL = Maximum = 2^levels - 1

mnemonics.title: Binary Tree Height Formula
mnemonics.content: For n nodes: Height = floor(log₂n) for complete tree | Max height = n-1 for skewed tree | Remember: COMPLETE = log, SKEWED = n-1

acronyms.usage: Used for efficient search, insert, delete operations O(log n) on average
Tree traversal that explores as far as possible before backtracking; includes preorder, inorder, postorder
Tree traversal that explores level by level; also called level order traversal
The deepest node that is an ancestor of both given nodes in a tree

visualTips.concept: Complete vs Full vs Perfect Binary Tree
visualTips.visualization: Draw 3 trees: (1) Complete: fill left-to-right like pyramid steps, (2) Full: every node has 0 or 2 children like full family, (3) Perfect: all levels full and all leaves at same level like perfect pyramid
Imagine a person walking: Preorder = stamp passport at entry, Inorder = meet person at their office, Postorder = clean up after leaving. Draw arrows showing the path order.
Think of family tree: Leaf = person with no children (end of lineage), Internal = parent with children. Draw circles for nodes, lines for edges.

analogies.analogy: Like the CEO of a company - the topmost person from whom all authority flows downward through managers to employees.
Like a fork in the road - each decision point splits into at most two paths (left or right).
Height is like measuring from top of building to ground (root to leaf), depth is like measuring from ground to a floor (root to node).
Like filling seats in a classroom row by row - fill all seats in current row before moving to next, and always fill from left.
